Documentation.Summary: Is a widget currently the fullscreen widget
Sets a widget as the fullscreen widget
Documentation.Return: whether or not the widget is now fullscreen

Documentation.Summary:
Opens an invisible popup above the game screen which allows you to left click once on the scene.
This is great for things like selecting something from the game scene.
Add a library from this folder
Install a library from a package. This will download the package and install it in the project's Library folder.
Get all active libraries
Remove and delete this library, and folder

Marks a method as a custom handler for stack trace lines matching a certain pattern.
The method must take in a <see cref="T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Match" /> parameter, and return
a <see cref="T:Editor.StackRow" /> (or null).
Register an object, start receiving events
Unregister an object, stop receiving events
Run an event.
Run an event with an argument of arbitrary type.

Params.name: Name of the event to run.
Params.arg0: Argument to pass down to event handlers.

Documentation.Summary: Run an event with 2 arguments of arbitrary type.

Params.name: Name of the event to run.
Params.arg0: First argument to pass down to event handlers.
Params.arg1: Second argument to pass down to event handlers.

TypeParams.T: Arbitrary type for the first argument.
TypeParams.U: Arbitrary type for the second argument.

Documentation.Summary: Run an event with 3 arguments of arbitrary type.

Params.name: Name of the event to run.
Params.arg0: First argument to pass down to event handlers.
Params.arg1: Second argument to pass down to event handlers.
Params.arg2: Third argument to pass down to event handlers.

TypeParams.T: Arbitrary type for the first argument.
TypeParams.U: Arbitrary type for the second argument.
TypeParams.V: Arbitrary type for the third argument.

Documentation.Summary: Called every frame for tools
Called when a property on a <see cref="T:Sandbox.GameObject" /> is about to be edited, so the old value can be inspected.
Called when a <see cref="T:Sandbox.GameObject" /> has been edited, so the new value can be inspected.
Called when a property on a <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> is about to be edited, so the old value can be inspected.
Called when a <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> has been edited, so the new value can be inspected.
Scene editor session that the context menu is being opened for.
Context menu being opened. Feel free to add options to it in your handler.
Cursor ray when right-click was pressed.
Trace result if we hit an object in the scene when right-clicking.
Event args for <see cref="M:Editor.EditorEvent.ISceneView.ShowContextMenu(Editor.EditorEvent.ShowContextMenuEvent)" /> events.

Params.Session: Scene editor session that the context menu is being opened for.
Params.Menu: Context menu being opened. Feel free to add options to it in your handler.
Params.CursorRay: Cursor ray when right-click was pressed.
Params.Trace: Trace result if we hit an object in the scene when right-clicking.

Documentation.Summary: Called when a scene editor viewport is drawing gizmos.

Params.scene: Scene that gizmos are being drawn for.

Documentation.Summary: Called when a scene editor viewport wants to show a context menu.

Params.ev: Event arguments describing what the context menu was opened on.

Documentation.Summary: Allows tools to inject behaviour in the scene editor.
Events that happen within the map editor.
Called when the user selects / deselects any object in the map and <see cref="P:Editor.MapEditor.Selection.All"></see> is changed.
Called when the map view is right clicked, <see cref="T:Editor.Menu" /> is passed.
